key_insight: VFEAgent automates the full finite element analysis workflow from images and text by combining multimodal reasoning with verification-first code generation.
engineering_relevance: This could reduce manual setup effort in engineering simulation and make FEA more accessible to users without deep domain expertise.
method: Uses a multimodal vision-language multi-agent pipeline to extract structured FEA specifications from heterogeneous inputs.
Applies ReAct-style reasoning to plan and refine the analysis workflow.
Generates simulation code with self-debugging, fallback, and validation checks to improve executability and physical correctness.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic evaluation across multiple engineering mechanics scenarios, with VFEAgent achieving a high success rate and outperforming LLM-based baselines in reliability and correctness.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate interpretation of input images and the robustness of automated code verification, so ambiguous geometry or physics assumptions may still require human review.
paper_title: VFEAgent: A Multimodal Agent Framework for End-to-End Automated Finite Element Analysis
